comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Landed (commit 8d96d658). Re-scoped per code investigation: overlays adopt the EXISTING if: skip-guard (no new IR field); the new piece is the deterministic overlay_triggers precompute step (9th built-in) computing keyword + structural trigger booleans an if: references, keeping the closed expression grammar untouched. Advisories addressed: E5 (trigger logic tested directly), G6 (committed: inclusion observable via the step's journaled trigger outputs + per-step durable skip status — no new run-start event). Code-review subagent (general-purpose): verified deps-graph shape (has_linked_* works), lint accepts the open-output reference; strengthened the replay test to a genuine store-edit (changed rebar.deps mock) proving the RECORDED output drives the replay decision. Contract schemas registered (fixed the schema-coverage gate). make lint clean; 308 workflow + 48 contract/coverage/overlay tests green.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS**

All five acceptance criteria are met by the implementation at the referenced commit.

**AC1 — Deterministic precompute step; expression grammar unchanged**
`overlay_triggers` is registered in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/steps.py` (lines 116–173). It computes all three trigger kinds: keyword/text substring match (lines 142–144), `has_children` (line 155), and `has_linked_<type>` (lines 156–172). The closed allow-list in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/lint_refs.py` (`_ALLOWED_EXPR`, lines 61–70) is untouched — five patterns only, no operators, comparisons, or regex.

**AC2 — Criterion INCLUDED or SKIPPED (durable event); covered by test**
`src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py` lines 317–331 show that a failed `if:` guard emits `status="skipped"` via `step_recorded`. `tests/unit/workflow/test_overlay_triggers.py` covers both outcomes: `test_criterion_included_when_trigger_truthy` (line 142) asserts `status == "succeeded"`, and `test_criterion_skipped_when_trigger_falsy` (line 154) asserts `status == "skipped"`.

**AC3 — REPLAY test: store edit does not change inclusion**
`test_inclusion_is_stable_under_a_store_edit_on_replay` (lines 183–218 of the test file) runs the workflow with `has_children=True`, records the outputs, then monkeypatches `rebar.deps` to return no children, re-runs with the SAME recorder, and asserts the recorded `has_children=True` is reused and `container_review` remains `succeeded`.

**AC4 — Resolved included-set is observable for coverage**
Documented in `docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md` lines 157–161: "Inclusion is recoverable without a new event vocabulary: `overlay_triggers`' booleans are journaled as its step output, and every criterion's `succeeded` vs `skipped` status is a durable `WORKFLOW_STEP` marker."

**AC5 — Overlay/criterion-inclusion mechanism documented; schemas registered**
Section 2.1 "Overlays: conditional criterion inclusion via `if:`" (docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md lines 124–161) documents the precompute-then-`if:` pattern with a YAML example. `src/rebar/schemas/overlay_triggers_input.schema.json` and `overlay_triggers_output.schema.json` exist and are registered in `CONTRACT_SCHEMAS` (`src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` lines 174–192).

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The reusable 4-pass review pipeline needs criteria conditionally INCLUDED based on the input (a
security criterion fires only on security triggers; intent-fidelity only when a session log is
linked; container criteria only for parents with children). INVESTIGATION FINDING (analyst, with
citations): the engine ALREADY has this mechanism — the per-step `if:` skip-guard (interpreter.py
`_guard_scoped`, v1, retained in v2). It does per-step conditional inclusion, evaluated over
run-start-journaled inputs + recorded prior outputs (frame scope), is replay-deterministic (no live
store access; a skipped step records a durable `skipped` WORKFLOW_STEP event), and fails closed.
So we must NOT add a redundant `when`/`enabled` field — `if:` IS the overlay mechanism.

## What (the real, narrow gap)

The closed expression grammar (`_ALLOWED_EXPR` in lint_refs.py; pinned by the v2 schema `expression`
) is a bare variable-reference language: NO operators, comparisons, functions, or regex — only
truthiness over `inputs.*` / `steps.*.outputs.*` / `loop.*` / `map.*`. So `if:` can branch on a
boolean already in scope but cannot COMPUTE one. The overlay triggers (has-children,
has-linked-session-log, security-keyword-in-plan) are computations. The clean design — keeping the
grammar deliberately tiny — is a DETERMINISTIC scripted pre-step that computes each trigger boolean
and emits it as an output; the criterion steps then carry `if: ${{ steps.triggers.outputs.<name> }}`.
This is the in-pattern shape (steps.py fetch_ticket/fetch_epic_graph). Plus, optionally, journal the
resolved INCLUDED-SET at run-start so coverage accounting reads one stable set (today inclusion is
implicit, inferable from per-step skip statuses).

## Scope

- A deterministic overlay-trigger precompute scripted-step (src/rebar/llm/workflow/steps.py) that
  turns overlay conditions (has-children, has-linked-session-log, keyword/trigger match over a text
  input) into boolean outputs an `if:` references. The expression grammar is UNCHANGED.
- Optionally: journal the resolved included-set into the run-start WORKFLOW_RUN event
  (executor.py run_started) so coverage reads a stable set; OR document that inclusion is recovered
  from per-step skip statuses if that is sufficient.
- Document `if:` as the overlay/criterion-inclusion mechanism (the precompute-then-guard pattern),
  and a test proving an input-determined criterion is included/excluded + a replay test that a
  mid-run store edit does not change inclusion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A deterministic precompute scripted-step computes overlay-trigger booleans (has-children,
      has-linked-session-log, text/keyword trigger match) as step outputs; the expression grammar is
      NOT extended (closed-allow-list preserved).
- [ ] A criterion (prompt) step gated by `if: ${{ steps.<triggers>.outputs.<name> }}` is INCLUDED
      when the trigger is truthy and SKIPPED (durable `skipped` event) when falsy — covered by a test.
- [ ] REPLAY test: after resolution, mutating the underlying store does NOT change the
      included/skipped outcome (inputs/outputs are read from the run-start journal, not live state).
- [ ] The resolved included-set is observable for coverage — either journaled into the run-start
      WORKFLOW_RUN event, or documented as recovered from per-step skip statuses (decide + record which).
- [ ] The overlay/criterion-inclusion mechanism (precompute-then-`if:`) is documented; make lint +
      the workflow test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: Overlay construct: a declarative when/enabled predicate on prompt steps (resolved set journaled at run-start)
